issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: Manage tokens between Multiple hosts (domains)
issue_body:
Right now, if the Django application is hosting multiple sites, a token generated from one site can be successfully verified and refreshed by another provided they share the same user/database.  This is not always desired. Django also manages session on a per-site basis.

While working on our multi tenant system in Retailephant.com, we faced this issue where tokens generated from a particular channel that we were hosting were successfully verified and refreshed by another.

What about a solution where the setting 'JWT_VERFIRY_HOST', with default = False, can be used to configure the desire behavior of verifying and refreshing a token based on the host?

issue_title: Crash trap with custom logger and sys.stdout.encoding
issue_body:
Start.c filed override `sys.stdout`, which has some properties set such as `encoding`. This opens a trap for anyone who will call `sys.stdout.encoding`, which I think is used even in some parts of the standard library.

A potential fix is [here](https://github.com/kivy/kivy/pull/5056), but that's only from Kivy's side and only applies for SDL2, therefore affected parts of p4a are:

* pygame
* webview
* service most likely too
* python only packages

An ugly fix might be to manually put `encoding = 'utf8'` into `LogFile`, but better one might be to try using the `sys.stdout` if there already is one and move its dictionary (variables) to our custom `LogFile` class and then apply our stuff. Then there'll be no such case of `sys.stdout.<random not available property>` won't be our fault and won't present a potential risk of crashing the whole application on some basic mistake e.g. while using a standard Python library.

issue_body:
A user reported this issue when attempting to import the python client:

```
$ pip install keen==0.3.6 
Downloading/unpacking keen==0.3.6 
Downloading keen-0.3.6.tar.gz 
Running setup.py (path:/home/dubizzle/webapps/price_monkey_env/build/keen/setup.py) egg_info for package keen

$ python 
Python 2.7.6 (default, Mar 22 2014, 22:59:56) 
[GCC 4.8.2] on linux2 
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information. 
>>> import keen 
Traceback (most recent call last): 
File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module> <br> File "/home/dubizzle/webapps/price_monkey_env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/keen/__init__.py", line 2, in &lt;module&gt; &lt;br&gt; from keen.client import KeenClient &lt;br&gt; File "/home/dubizzle/webapps/price_monkey_env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/keen/client.py", line 6, in &amp;lt;module&amp;gt; &amp;lt;br&amp;gt; from keen.api import KeenApi &amp;lt;br&amp;gt; File "/home/dubizzle/webapps/price_monkey_env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/keen/api.py", line 8, in &amp;amp;lt;module&amp;amp;gt; &amp;amp;lt;br&amp;amp;gt; from requests.packages.urllib3.poolmanager import PoolManager &amp;amp;lt;br&amp;amp;gt;ImportError: No module named packages.urllib3.poolmanager&amp;amp;lt;/module&amp;amp;gt;&amp;lt;/module&amp;gt;&lt;/module&gt;</module></stdin>
```

From what I can tell from [this issue](https://github.com/mk-fg/python-onedrive/issues/13), which was supposedly fixed almost a year ago, it seems like requests has issues importing urllib3. I suspect if we simply require urllib3 and import PoolManager from there it will fix this. When I looked at the [code base for requests 2.2.1](https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/tree/33735480f77891754304e7f13e3cdf83aaaa76aa), it looks like they have copied over the code from urllib3 rather than importing it, and I can't tell what version of urllib3 they expect, so I'm confused.

I can just add urllib3 to requirements.txt and test it, but if anyone has any other ideas please let me know!

issue_title: Please don't import eventlet even if it's available
issue_body:
This code has two problems:
```python
try:
    import eventlet
    try:
        from eventlet.green import urllib2 as eventlet_urllib2
    except ImportError:
        from eventlet.green.urllib import request as eventlet_urllib2
    has_eventlet = True
except:
    has_eventlet = False
```

1. Bare `except:` is evil, please catch a specific exception, ImportError in this case.
2. I am trying to get rid of eventlet in my code base, and so I have an assertion in my code: `assert 'eventlet' not in sys.modules`.  Unconditionally importing eventlet makes it fail that assertion.  Instead, could you please get the module only if it's already imported?  `if 'eventlet' in sys.modules: stuff`.

issue_title: How to timeout captureException
issue_body:
Maybe there is another solution to this problem.

We have a django view that in some part of the code has a:

```
try:
...
except Exception:
    # we want the view to log to sentry but continue since the code inside the `try` block is not essential
    sentry_client.captureException()
```

We had a bug that caused an exception on every new request. I might be wrong, but it seems like `captureException` locks until a previous `captureException` call is done. This causes all requests to delay and end up adding up to absurd response times.

Is there a way to set a timeout the `captureException` or defer that to a celery task?

We are running `raven 6.0.0`.

Would really appreciate some guidance on how to solve this problem.

Thanks!

issue_title: Consistency Namespace vs. ClosedNamespace
issue_body:
As I understand rdflib.Namespace and ClosedNamespace, they are both meant as auxiliaries to simplify the creation of URIRefs in Python code, e.g. `RDF.type` or `OWL.Class`. In addition, ClosedNamespace provides an additional vocabulary check, e.g., `RDF.spam` will raise an exception.

However, sometimes other uses of Namespace/ClosedNamespace can be useful, e.g., string comparison. At this stage, it is quite inconvenient that Namespace is derived from unicode, whereas ClosedNamespace is derived from the standard object, and the actual namespace string is an instance attribute (uri). In the interest of consistency, I propose that ClosedNamespace should also be derived from unicode (or even from Namespace).

Regards

issue_title: Decrease max_connections parameter.
Heads-up: Etcd3 will require significant changes
issue_body:
Etcd3 will require significant changes for compatibility:

1. protobufs and base-64 encoded keys mean that we can't use the same keying;
2. elimination of tree structures for a flat keyspace means that we have to change how we name the keys

Possibly python drivers will handle this for us, but maybe not.

https://coreos.com/blog/etcd3-a-new-etcd.html

issue_title: Support socket connections for local patroni instance
issue_body:
Currently, the local patroni instance can only connect using TCP.  We should support local unix socket connections as well; that's both faster and more secure.

This will require some replumbing, which I regard as a cleanup anyway.  Currently, we read config information, use that to create a URL, then parse that URL to get the config information back.  This seems unnecessarily ornate; we should either use URLs in the config in the first place, or we should avoid this whole unnecessary step and use the config fields directly.  For backwards-compatibility reasons, I plan to use the latter path.

I'll also use a file path for the socket interface if that's preferred by the user.  It should  be simple to differentiate this from an IPv4/v6 address.

I plan to do this in the next couple of days, so let me know if you have comments on the design.

issue_title: Add more parameter validation on type:array
issue_body:
### Description
When using a parameter like:
````
  dow:
    name: dow
    description: An array of days of the week (0 - 6)
    in: query
    required: true
    type: array
    items:
      type: integer
      minimum: 0
      maximum: 6
      maxItems: 7
      minItems: 1
    collectionFormat: csv
````
Validation doesn't occur given the properties (minimum, maximum, maxItems, minItems) that are in the https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#itemsObject


### Expected behaviour
Validation on that properties should exists


### Actual behaviour
Validation doesn't occur


### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `3-6-1`
- `connexion=1.1.15`

issue_title: Implement persistence in broker
issue_body:
Currently, all sessions data managed by a broker are stored in memory. This is a limit for serious use. 
HBMQTT broker should be able to persist sessions data (while offline).
Implementation should be based on the plugin system which would allow to use different persistence engine.

issue_title: checksum when sending "data object" to iRODS
issue_body:
Is there a way (using this python binding) to either
* provide a checksum when sending an object to irods ?
* once an object has been sent to irods, ask irods to compute (and store) a checksum ?

Using command line, there's an option to "iput" so that a checksum is computed at the origin, another one in irods, and they are compared. This enhance reliability of the transport inside irods from an external source.

It is possible the other way around, when getting an object. If a checksum is already computed/stored by irods, it is provided as ".checksum" on the python object, and we do already use this for checking the file integrity once received. What we are aiming here is to get the same level of reliability in the other direction.

issue_title: Issue Modifying API Gateway Authorizers
issue_body:
A week or two ago, I created an authorizer on the API Gateway via a call like:

authorizer = CognitoUserPoolAuthorizer(
    'My Auth Name', header='Authorization',
    provider_arns=['my provider settings'])

In the meantime, I deleted and recreated the Cognito User Pool that was associated with this authorizer. I updated my provider_arns in the code and redeployed, expecting that the existing Authorizer would be updated. But it isn't-I had to go into the API Gateway panel and manually edit/delete the authorizer, then redeploy with chalice, before things started working again.

It's understandable that the existing resource isn't updated by "chalice deploy", although it would be nice to have that. But it would be better if the documentation was more explicit that changes in code to CognitoUserPoolAuthorizers that have already been created via chalice will not be updated on the API Gateway via further chalice deploys. Or, I wouldn't mind if the "chalice deploy" failed on encountering an authorizer on the gateway that is inconsistent with the authorizer specified in code--I lost a few hours to figuring out why my Auth calls weren't working this evening.

Thanks!

issue_title: Blob.upload_from_file doesn't work for file-like objects without size parameter
issue_body: When using ``StringIO``, ``io.BytesIO`` or other file-like objects (such as Flask/werkzeug's file upload object), trying to upload via ``Blob.upload_from_file`` doesn't work because it attempts to find the size of the object using ``os.stat`` throwing a ``AttributeError: StringIO instance has no attribute 'fileno'``. Passing in the size manually fixes this, but imo this inconsistency shouldn't burden the user as there are other ways than fileno to determine the size of a StringIO or io.BytesIO instance (``seek``, ``tell``).

issue_title: Tagging Strategy with sub-packages
issue_body:
I used the "question" label but this is more like an informative issue. Supersedes #2462.

Tag names will be as follows:

- `{pkg}-0.x.y` for releases to the `google-cloud-{pkg}` PyPI package, e.g. `core-0.21.1`, `datastore-0.22.0`, etc.
- `0.x.y` for releases of the umbrella package (as we had already been doing)

----

- For [hand-rolled stable][2] builds (i.e. `gh-pages`), we'll have a custom script which install from the latest version of each package on PyPI, rather than from the source tree (this may be a chicken-and-egg problem on tag builds that would both push docs **and** push to PyPI)
- We will be disabling [RTD stable][1] builds: we can't have local filesystem [deps][3] on `latest` builds and then have PyPI deps on `stable` builds (RTD [subprojects][4] may help)

[1]: http://google-cloud-python.readthedocs.io/en/stable/
[2]: https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/stable/
[3]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/6916814c94f6329ce036c01093e7728961c24789/docs/requirements.txt
[4]: http://read-the-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/faq.html#how-do-i-host-multiple-projects-on-one-cname

issue_title: How should we pass options for http clients in service client creation?
issue_body:
In order to address #2988, where timeouts are not a service option, but need to be handled by the HTTP client.

How should we handle passing arguments like `timeout` when creating a service client?

```python
client = someservice.Client(timeout=10)
```

This value could then be passed [here](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/14c250316aeef72b7f951a0959c46855948bd2b7/core/google/cloud/client.py#L118-L139) but we would need to assume the argument was available.

In this case [`google_auth_httplib2.AuthorizedHttp`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-auth-library-python/blob/d840beceafacd37a6abf993cd1fb8039eb3b6864/httplib2_transport/google_auth_httplib2.py#L147) doesn't take the argument but rather an already created http client.

We could make another mixin class for `Client` I suppose that supports setting `timeout`?

issue_title: hostname and hosts entries not working on CentOS7 w/o NetworkManager
issue_body:
Unsure if this is also broken in CentOS6 and other derivatives but if you have NetworkManager disabled, the google-compute-engine script installed at /etc/dhcp/dhclient.d/google_hostname.sh does not function correctly.  While /etc/resolv.conf is updated properly, the system hostname, /etc/hosts entries and metadata hosts entries are never installed.  

The root cause appears to be that google_hostname.sh calls /bin/set_hostname without any path:
````
google_hostname_config() {
  set_hostname
}
````
and the redhat network-scripts environment defines a function called set_hostname, which winds up conflicting:
````
 grep set_hostname -R /etc/*
/etc/dhcp/dhclient.d/google_hostname.sh:  /bin/set_hostname
/etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/network-functions:set_hostname ()
/etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifup-post:	    set_hostname $HOSTNAME
````

The net result is that google's /bin/set_hostname is never called.  Changing google_hostname.sh to reference set_hostname with a full path fixes the issue.  It might be better to just rename set_hostname to something that is clearly a google specific script, however.

issue_title: sync needs lock for multiple objects writing to archive?
issue_body: some of the archives handle multiple writes by providing a lock, some don't.  Should `klepto` provide a lock where the backends themselves don't?

issue_body:

`PicklingError: Can't pickle <function <lambda> at 0x1187952f0>: attribute lookup <lambda> on jupyter_client.session failed`

As my script is already very complex I just show the function which contains the parallelization. All functions are defined at top-level.

    #!/usr/bin/env python3
    # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

    mport scipy.io

    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    from tqdm import tqdm
    
    class catch2grid(object):
        """See module docstring."""

    def __init__(self):
        """Init of catch2grid."""
        self.pbar = None

...

                 l):
        """Parallel computing of several flow percentiles for Qobs and Qsim,
        the standard deviation of the flow percentiles of Qsim and the
        KGE alpha.

        db_Qobs_meta_dir -- Path to file with meta informations on the
                            Catchments
        ens_mean_dir -- Path to file with runoff ensemble mean
        ens_sd_dir -- Path to file with runoff ensemble standard deviation
        db_Qobs_dir -- Path to folder with observed runoff database_Qobs_new
        """
        cpu_cores = mpp.cpu_count() - 1
        df_meta = self.import_meta(db_Qobs_meta_dir)
        df_meta = self.select_catchments(df_meta)
        # chunking subsets for parallelization
        subsets = []
        lin_dist = np.linspace(0, l, cpu_cores+1)

        for i in range(len(lin_dist) - 1):

        res_kgealpha = []
        # collect dataframes and merge them
        [res_obs.append(res[:][i][0]) for i in range(len(res))]
        [res_sim.append(res[:][i][1]) for i in range(len(res))]
        [res_simsd.append(res[:][i][2]) for i in range(len(res))]
        [res_kgealpha.append(res[:][i][3]) for i in range(len(res))]
        df_Qobs_percs = pd.concat(res_obs[:], ignore_index=True)
        df_Qsim_percs = pd.concat(res_sim[:], ignore_index=True)
        df_sdQsim_percs = pd.concat(res_simsd[:], ignore_index=True)
        df_KGE_alpha = pd.concat(res_kgealpha[:], ignore_index=True)
        
        return df_Qobs_percs, df_Qsim_percs, df_sdQsim_percs, df_KGE_alpha
        if __name__ == "__main__":
            start_time = time.time()  # measuring computation time
            c2g = catch2grid()
            c2g.init_pbar(l)
            df_Qobs_percs, df_Qsim_percs, df_sdQsim_percs, df_KGE_alpha = \
            c2g.main_par(db_Qobs_meta_dir, Ens_mean_dir, Ens_sd_dir, db_Qobs_dir, l)
            time_elapsed = time.time() - start_time
            print('Parallel run: Time elapsed (hh.mm) {}'.format(time_elapsed/

issue_title: win10 64位家庭版遇到自动休眠的奇葩问题
issue_body:
近日突然出现如下症状：在pac模式下未使用墙外地址一段时间后，SS貌似进入了休眠状态，无法翻墙。日志上也没有任何的记录。没有上行与下行流量。在随意更改点击菜单中任何一项后恢复正常（如更新PAC文件、更改服务器再改回来，在PAC与全局中切换下）。服务端应该是正常的，链接同一服务器的其他电脑都没有问题。
一开始我以为是迅雷XLServicePlatform的锅，禁用后问题依旧。
之后我发现XLServicePlatform会自己重新启动，遂卸载迅雷，问题依旧。
在网上找了很久，貌似没有碰到过与我有一样症状的。求高人指点，感激不尽。

issue_title: Sql management will support it?
Feature request: support for multi line strings given to Fn::Sub
issue_body:
I'm passing a multi line string to user data like this:

```
x = """
This is
some
user data
"""

....
UserData(Base64(Sub(x)))
```

I'm getting output like this:

```
UserData: !Base64
  Fn::Sub: "\nThis is\nsome\nuser data\n"
```

I kinda expected this:

```
UserData:
  Base64:
    Fn::Sub: |
      This is
      some
      user data
```

I suspect this is partially related to the json/yaml processors and probably not something you can fix, but I thought I'd ask anyway.

issue_title: First example is docs doesn't work (or I'm understanding it wrong?)
issue_body:
https://pex.readthedocs.org/en/stable/buildingpex.html says:

> The easiest way to build .pex files is with the pex utility, which is made available when you pip install pex. Do this within a virtualenv, then you can use pex to bootstrap itself:
> ```
> $ pex pex requests -c pex -o ~/bin/pex
> ```
> This command creates a pex file containing pex and requests, using the console script named “pex”, saving it in ~/bin/pex. At this point, assuming ~/bin is on your $PATH, then you can use pex in or outside of any virtualenv.

But when I try building a .pex that way, it isn't able to `import requests`:
```
$ pex pex requests -c pex -o /tmp/pex
$ /tmp/pex
Python 2.7.9 (default, Feb 10 2015, 03:29:19)
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.0 (clang-600.0.56)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
(InteractiveConsole)
>>> import requests
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<console>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: No module named requests
```

Using pex-1.3.0:
```
$ pip show pex
---
Metadata-Version: 2.0
Name: pex
Version: 1.0.3
Summary: The PEX packaging toolchain.
Home-page: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex
Author: UNKNOWN

Location: /usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Requires: setuptools
```

issue_title: Support checkstyle extensions better.
issue_body:
Right now, the [checkstyle tool is shaded](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/checkstyle.py#L42-L56), although the `com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle` package is excluded from shading to keep module names as-documented by checkstyle.  This arrangment does not help custom extension added to the classpath though.  Those wil be shaded and users then must adapt their coding_style.xml like so:

Before
```xml
<module name="Checker">
    <module name="com.twitter.common.checkstyle.SplitSuppressionFilter">
      <property name="files" value="${checkstyle.suppression.files}"/>
    </module>

    <module name="SuppressionCommentFilter">
      <property name="offCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:OFF ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="onCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:ON ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="checkFormat" value="$1"/>
    </module>
...
```

After
```xml
<module name="Checker">
    <module name="__shaded_by_pants__.com.twitter.common.checkstyle.SplitSuppressionFilter">
      <property name="files" value="${checkstyle.suppression.files}"/>
    </module>

    <module name="SuppressionCommentFilter">
      <property name="offCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:OFF ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="onCommentFormat" value="CHECKSTYLE\:ON ([\w\|]+)"/>
      <property name="checkFormat" value="$1"/>
    </module>
...
```

Someone configuring pants should not need to know or figure out the magic `__shaded_by_pants__` package prefix added by the pants tool shading.  The should be able to opt their checker package(s) out of shading instead in some way.

issue_title: Support bringing a JDK along for remote execution
issue_body:
Right now, we have the `jdk_home` property on `ExecuteProcessRequest` which for local execution will make a `.jdk` directory pointing at that directory, and for remote execution will request that `.jdk` be symlinked to a system-installed JDK.

Instead, we should include the JDK as part of the input directory. This is non-trivial, partially because the JDK is large, but mostly because the JDK is platform specific, and we don't have a good way to distribute it.

https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/pants#L41 needs quotes:

```

issue_title: Allow looking up buildcache hosts in pants
issue_body:
This is to propose an implementation to the problem described in [1]. 

In short, today buildcache urls are statically configured in pants. In Twitter case we use a centralized load balancer, that becomes a bottleneck with the recent monobuild launch. Deploying a local proxy is a possibility but not quite ready yet because we have heterogeneous deployment environments besides aurora, jenkins for example.

Proposal here is to add options *resolve-read-from* and *resolve-write-to* to [CacheSetup] (https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/347e68a37421aa2282c31a07559dcb28b7e52e64/src/python/pants/cache/cache_setup.py), each of which may point to an external http endpoint that supplies buildcache urls.

pseudo code looks something like this:
```
read_from_urls = list(self.options.read_from)
if self.options.resolve_read_from:
    read_from_urls.extend(resolver.resolve(self.options.resolve_read_from))

// the rest logic remain unchanged
best_url = select_best_url(pinger, read_from_urls)
...
```

Ideally the resolved urls are updated periodically to keep them fresh during the lifespan of pants run, but this is something I feel can leave as a future improvement.

[1] [pants-devel discussion](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/LL6jICXSniU).

issue_title: `./pants list` with no targets does not render a warning/error
issue_body:
The behaviour of `./pants list` has changed between v1 and v2, and for a while a warning existed to indicate to users of a no-arg `list` that its behaviour would be changing soon.

It looks like that warning must have been removed at some point, because I don't see a warning with either v1 or v2 in the `1.3.x` branch:
```
./pants --enable-v2-engine list | head
./pants --no-enable-v2-engine list | head
```

issue_title: Open source
issue_body:
I'd like to request making the underlying implementation of the Serverless Application Model available under the same [LICENSE](https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/blob/master/LICENSE) that governs the specification distributed in this repository.

This would help users by giving them the ability to inspect the underlying implementation to help with debugging unexpected behavior and edge-cases. It would help the project by making it possible for external contributors interested in supporting and advancing this project to submit pull requests with bug and documentation fixes, new tests and feature implementations, etc.

issue_title: Custom table style creates adds borders
issue_body:
I created a table style called 'MyGrid' in a word document. The style is based on Table Grid. The style differs from Table Grid in three ways: it has no borders, it has arial 10 as the font, and it has zero margins on the cells. When I run the following code the  output docx (test_table_style) respects all of those changes, except borders. The output file has borders on the table. Also when I re-open the original template file the MyGrid style has borders again.
```
doc = docx.Document('template.docx')
tbl = doc.add_table(1,2)
c = tbl.cell(0, 0)
para = c.paragraphs[0]
top = para.add_run('Cell 1')
c = tbl.cell(0,1)
para = c.paragraphs[0]
run = para.add_run('Cell 2')
tbl.style = 'MyGrid'
doc.save('test_table_style.docx')
```

issue_title: Django 2 compatibility
issue_body:
Hi,
I found a problem in this file
```
  File "/.../lib/python3.6/site-packages/nocaptcha_recaptcha/client.py", line 8, in <module>
    from django.utils import simplejson as json

```
This is trying to import simplejson (not available since Django 1.5) instead of json package, because of this test, that checks Django only checks the subversion : 

```
if django.VERSION[1] >= 5:
    import json
else:
    from django.utils import simplejson as json
```
By replacing the first line with this, this is ok :
`if django.VERSION[1] >= 5 or django.VERSION[0] > 1:`

Please update it, that would be great :)

Thanks, 
Louison

issue_title: api client 'reverse_geocode' does not support place_ids
issue_body:
The Google Maps Reverse Geocoding service allows for the retrieval of a place's address details based on a place_id. The current implementation does not support this feature of the Geocoding service.

There are no methods builtin to over-ride the default query which is done based on lat/lng co-ordinates.

Currrently the only way to support this functionality of the Geocoding service is to refactor the existing code, with my local implementation being able to do do both, with a place_id receiving a higher priority if included in the args, else the default lat/lng lookup.

Was the excluion of queries by place_id, a conscious design decision.

I think this feature should have been included, considering Google's value outlook on place_id's vs any other metric for addressing place's in the Geocoding service.

File: googlemaps/geocoding.py:71

issue_title: hover state on cards is slightly jarring
issue_body:
The cards currently transition from a small drop-shadow to a large one on hover:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/26953519-a481ca34-4c5f-11e7-9d19-208abdd88e36.png) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/26953508-984260c6-4c5f-11e7-9c45-f35e0e87aba3.png)

The instantaneous transition is a bit jarring, because drop-shadows imply a physical elevation and it's impossible for something to instantaneously move from one elevation to another. Recommend applying an eased transition.

issue_title: print detailed exception info to server console on 500 errors
issue_body:
Currently, the web server middleware swallows all Python exceptions and returns the traceback information to the client in a 500 error. This makes debugging difficult.

It should be printed to the console and saved to log files.

issue_body:
The PyMISP version string uses major.minor.misp-pl,pymisp-pl which causes the following warning:

```
The version of PyMISP recommended by the MISP instance (2.4.81) is older than the one you're using now (2.4.81.2). Please upgrade the MISP instance or use an older PyMISP version.
```

A simple solution would be to strip out the last (4th) pymisp-pl part of the version before comparing.

issue_title: Invalid 'Rate-limit' and 'Value' in JSON output for extended communities
issue_body:
ISSUE TYPE
Bug Report

OS
Ubuntu 16.04.5 (Debian/Linux)

VERSION
```
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 3.5.2 (default, Nov 23 2017, 16:37:01)  [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
Uname  : Linux exabgp01 4.4.0-101-generic #124-Ubuntu SMP Fri Nov 10 18:29:59 UTC 2017 x86_64
Root   : /opt/exabgp

```

ENVIRONMENT 
```
[exabgp.api]
encoder = text
respawn = false

[exabgp.daemon]
user = 'root'

[exabgp.log]
destination = '/var/log/exabgp.log'
level = DEBUG
routes = true


```

CONFIGURATION

```

process syslog {
        run /usr/bin/python /home/path/to/script.py;
        encoder json;
}


neighbor 192.168.194.100 {
        description "some-description";
        hold-time 180;
        listen 179;
        peer-as 65534;
        router-id 192.168.194.200;
        local-as 65534;
        local-address 192.168.194.200;
    api {
        processes [ syslog ];
        neighbor-changes;
        receive {
            parsed;
            update;
        }
    }

    }

```


SUMMARY
The exabgp is configured to receive messages from a BGP peer in the JSON format. For BGP Update (Flowspec) messages, the JSON contains key-value pair for the key - 'extended-community' containing values like 'rate-limit' and 'value'. Firstly, the rate-limit always shows as '0' in spite of the peer being configured to send a non-zero rate limit. Secondly, the 'value' which contains the decimal representation of the packet has a 'L' appended to end of the integer value. This creates issues when processing the JSON, since a value with a letter is seen as 'string' but results in an error since it does not have 'quotes' around it. For example: "value":  9225340944352543000L


STEPS TO REPRODUCE

issue_title: ExaBGP 4 and python 3 support
issue_body:
Currently, ExaBGP 4 is supporting python 3 and python 2 (even though it should not be relied upon).

> ExaBGP 4.0 and later are targeting python3 when ExaBGP 3.4 and previous versions are python2 applications. At the moment, it is possible to run ExaBGP 4.0 using python2.7 but this backward compatibility should not be relied upon.

While in many cases supporting both version can be straightforward, I believe that for ExaBGP case, supporting py3 only would make things easier has:
* you wont be forced in supporting slight language differences
* bytes vs strings will be much more straightforward
* the event loop could move to a natively supported framework (asyncio) vs building your own
* IP parsing could benefit from ipaddress module which ships by default with py3
* testing also becomes easier

Overall, python 3 is widely deployed in most distros. Python 3.5 supports the new syntactic sugar that makes asyncio easier, adds type hints support.

I appreciate that python 2.7 could be supported, but I think the project would benefit from only supporting 1 python version for simplicity.
Not a bug, but just wanted to raise this in case.

issue_title: Support bracket layers in Glyphs
issue_body:
Bracket layers in Glyphs are a very convenient mechanism for drawing alternates for glyphs. It would be very helpful if fontmake supported Glyphs' bracket layers, storing the layers as additional glyphs in the glyf table, and generating the necessary rvrn feature and Feature Variations code in GSUB. 

This could be implemented using Rules in the .designspace file. Fontmake would just generate the rules append the alternate glyphs to the glyf table. Fonttools.varLIb could consume it all and generate the FeatureVariations code. This would also free us up from depending on feature file syntax.

issue_body:
Example: NI-FGEN function:

```
    def get_fir_filter_coefficients(self):
        '''get_fir_filter_coefficients

        | Returns the FIR filter coefficients used by the onboard signal
          processing block. These coefficients are determined by NI-FGEN and
          based on the FIR filter type and corresponding attribute (Alpha,
          Passband, BT) unless you are using the custom filter. If you are using
          a custom filter, the coefficients returned are those set with the
          nifgen_ConfigureCustomFIRFilterCoefficients function coerced to the
          quantized values used by the device.
        | To use this function, first call an instance of the
          get_fir_filter_coefficients function with the
          **coefficientsArray** parameter set to VI_NULL. Calling the function
          in this state returns the current size of the **coefficientsArray** as
          the value of the **numberOfCoefficientsRead** parameter. Create an
          array of this size, and call the get_fir_filter_coefficients
          function a second time, passing the new array as the
          **coefficientsArray** parameter and the size as the **arraySize**
          parameter. This second function call populates the array with the FIR
          filter coefficients.
        | Refer to the FIR Filter topic for your device in the *NI Signal
          Generators Help* for more information about FIR filter coefficients.
          This function is supported only for the NI 5441.
        | **Default Value**: None

        Tip:
        This method requires repeated capabilities (usually channels). If called directly on the
        nifgen.Session object, then the method will use all repeated capabilities in the session.
        You can specify a subset of repeated capabilities using the Python index notation on an
        nifgen.Session instance, and calling this method on the result.:

            session['0,1'].get_fir_filter_coefficients()

        Args:
            array_size (int): Specifies the size of the coefficient array

        Returns:
            number_of_coefficients_read (int): Specifies the array of data containing the number of coefficients you
                want to read.
        '''
```

It should have an additional "coefficients_array" return value, before number_of_coefficients_read.

issue_title: Protocol error while handling subscribe error
issue_body:
I'm publish message from the latest `autobahn-js` to a `autobahn-python` subscriber. The router is not crossbar, but a wamprpc 2.0 implementation `wamp-server`. The python code uses `serializers=[JsonSerializer()]`.

Now the python subscriber receives a message like this
```
    [36, 8483690563665722, 8483690563665722, 'testpy.echo', [], {}]
```
But I met the error
```
  File "C:\Users\xusta\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\autobahn\wamp\message.py", line 274, in check_or_raise_extra
    raise ProtocolError(u"{0}: invalid type {1}".format(message, type(value)))
autobahn.wamp.exception.ProtocolError: 'details' in EVENT: invalid type <class 'str'>
```
This is how I subscribe
```
    client.subscribe(shutdown, topic="testpy.shutdown", options=SubscribeOptions(details=False))
```


The 4th parameter is topic. I see the [WAM RPC spec](http://wamp-proto.org/static/rfc/draft-oberstet-hybi-crossbar-wamp.html#rfc.section.9.2) support to be a string.
```
    [16, 239714735, {}, "com.myapp.mytopic1", [], {"color": "orange",
        "sizes": [23, 42, 7]}]
```

In `autobbahn\wamp\message.py` line 2439, the 4th parameter is not topic but details
```
        # my proposed workaround
        # if type(wmsg[3]) == str:
        #     wmsg[3] = dict(topic=wmsg[3])

        details = check_or_raise_extra(wmsg[3], u"'details' in EVENT")
```
It requires this param to be a dict, like this `{topic: "'testpy.echo"}`. Is it possible to accept a string arg? I currently use a workaround. But I think autobahnjs and autobahnpython should be able to communciate very well.

issue_title: Unexpected relation between pitch attributes and frequency or pitchspace?
issue_body:
My apologies if this question is inappropriate here, but I couldn't find a mailing list or the like.

Consider the following testing code.

```python
from music21 import pitch
    
C0 = 16.35
    
for f in [261, 130, 653, 64, 865]:
    p = pitch.Pitch()
    p.frequency = f
       
    # Compare manual frequency with music21 frequency
    f1 = p.frequency
    f2 = C0 * pow(2, p.octave) * pow(2, p.pitchClass / 12) * pow(2, p.microtone.cents / 1200)
    print(f, f1, f2)
    
    # Compare manual pitchspace with music21 pitchspace
    ps1 = p.ps
    ps2 = 12 * (p.octave + 1) + p.pitchClass + p.microtone.cents / 100
    print(ps1, ps2)
    print()
```

The output of this is

    261 260.99999402174154 521.9489797003519
    59.958555 71.95855499999999
    
    130 129.99999854289362 259.974590631057
    47.892097 59.892097
    
    653 653.0000144741496 652.9362051837928
    75.834954 75.834954
    
    64 63.999998381902046 65.86890433005668
    35.623683 36.123683
    
    865 864.9999846113213 890.2594167561009
    80.702359 81.202359

There is often a difference between my manual computation of the frequency resp. the pitch space and the `music21` value.
Note that sometimes this difference can be about an octave (like the first two C note frequencies), but mostly it is about one tone. Another weird thing is that for the third testing frequency the pitchspace values are the same while the frequencies are not.

Are my manual formulas wrong, or is `music21` using the attributes in an unexpected way?

issue_title: Apps should be published with an descriptive hostname
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm currently testing out New Relic for monitoring applications we have running on Deis, and all the instances (JVMs) show up with names like this: `1ecbec0cb893`, since that is the Docker hostname.

What I would rather prefer, is a hostname like`my-application.cmd.1.deis.example.com` or  `my-application.cmd.1`, since this would make looking up the actual instance in New Relic easier.

I think the solution would be to use Dockers `--hostname=` parameter in `docker run` ( https://docs.docker.com/articles/networking/#dns ).

>`-h HOSTNAME` or `--hostname=HOSTNAME` — sets the hostname by which the container knows itself. This is written into `/etc/hostname`, into `/etc/hosts` as the name of the container's host-facing IP address, and is the name that `/bin/bash` inside the container will display inside its prompt. But the hostname is not easy to see from outside the container. It will not appear in docker ps nor in the `/etc/hosts` file of any other container.

Any thoughts?

issue_title: endpoint_id:path vs. endpoint_id/path
issue_body:
In the ssh CLI we deprecated the SCP-like : separator between endpoint_id and path.  So this is deprecated:
af7bda53-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec:/~/myproject

Instead it should be a URL-like separator of /, such as:
af7bda53-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec/~/myproject

This URL-like separator is also more consistent with where endpoints are going with HTTPS, as every endpoint will have a DNS name, with URL path naming.  We should adopt this same style consistently throughout the new CLI.

issue_title: Implement `State` object
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently, the `VM` class is part of the constructor for the `Computation` object.  Here are the places where it is currently used.

```
evm/logic/block.py:7:    block_hash = computation.vm.get_ancestor_hash(block_number)
evm/logic/block.py:13:    computation.stack.push(computation.vm.block.header.coinbase)
evm/logic/block.py:17:    computation.stack.push(computation.vm.block.header.timestamp)
evm/logic/block.py:21:    computation.stack.push(computation.vm.block.header.block_number)
evm/logic/block.py:25:    computation.stack.push(computation.vm.block.header.difficulty)
evm/logic/block.py:29:    computation.stack.push(computation.vm.block.header.gas_limit)
evm/logic/system.py:87:    computation.vm.logger.debug(
evm/logic/system.py:141:            computation.vm.logger.debug(
evm/vm/forks/spurious_dragon/utils.py:22:        yield computation.vm.block.header.coinbase
evm/vm/computation.py:76:        self.vm = vm
evm/vm/computation.py:197:            child_computation = self.vm.apply_create_message(child_msg)
evm/vm/computation.py:199:            child_computation = self.vm.apply_message(child_msg)
evm/vm/computation.py:280:        with self.vm.state_db(read_only, self.msg.access_list) as state_db:
```

Giving computation direct access to the `VM` is problematic for a number of reasons.

- poor isolation for what data and APIs are available during EVM execution.
- makes implementation of stateless clients difficult.
- makes VM execution inherently non-pure.

### How can it be fixed

To fix this, lets create a new `State` object.  Similar to how the `Transaction` class is specified for each `VM`, the `State` object should be similarly overrideable.

- `VM.get_state_class()`
- `VM.get_state()`
- `VM.state` (this could be a convenience `property` that just delegates to `VM.get_state()`.

The `State` object will need the following things.

- Access to the current block header information (`coinbase/timestamp/block_number/difficulty/gas_limit`.
- The ancestry hashes for the current block number.
- Access to the `StateDB`

The `State` object will also need the following APIs available to it.

- `VM.apply_message`
- `VM.apply_create_message`

I think the best way to accomplish this is to relocate the following APIs to live directly on the `State` object itself, removing them from the `VM` object.

- `VM.apply_message`
- `VM.apply_create_message`
- `VM.apply_computation`

Note that `VM.apply_computation` needs access to `VM.get_opcode_fn()` and `VM.precompiles`.  This indicates we'll need to supply the `State` object with a way to get at this data.  The *simplest* solution that comes to mind is to add them as extra arguments:

```python
class State:
    def apply_computation(self, message, opcodes, precompiles):
        ...
```

issue_title: Meta: Overhaul label system
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The way we currently use labels could be improved to enable better issue discovery.

### How can it be fixed

I'd like to overhaul and unify the labels in this repo and potentially apply the same rules to other repos as well. This is inspired by https://github.com/machinelabs/machinelabs which in turn is inspired by https://github.com/angular/angular

Here are two secreenshots to give an idea what it could look like.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/521109/42214872-ff6b4098-7ebc-11e8-9101-fd20fc710f82.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/521109/42214885-0770b4d0-7ebd-11e8-82bc-760d02d9a545.png)

The gist of it goes like this.

We would create labels that fall into the following categories:

`comp: <component-name>` (e.g. `comp: p2p`, `comp: trinity`, `comp: benchmark`)
`type: <type>` (e.g. `type: feature`, `type: bug`, `type: docs`)
`effort: <effort-estimate>` (e.g. `effort: hours`, `effort: days`, `effort: weeks`)
`prio: <prio>` (e.g. `prio: P0-critical`, `prio: P1-urgent`, `prio: P2-required`)
`PR-state: <state>` (e.g. `PR-state: wip`, `PR-state: needs review`, `PR-state: blocked`)

All the labels that fall into the same category share the same color (as seen on the screenshot). Nothing set in stone regarding concrete labels but I like the core idea of it. I'm happy to do the migration work we want to go that way.

    {
        'fromBlock': 0,
        'toBlock': 'latest',
    })
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
try:
    task = loop.create_task(
        self.log_loop(certificate_created_filter, 0.5))
    loop.run_until_complete(task)
finally:
    loop.close()
```
_Checking for events_
```python
    async def log_loop(self, event_filter, poll_interval):
        event_filter.get_all_entries()
        while True:
            for event in event_filter.get_new_entries():
                self.handle_event(event)
            await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
```

```python
for _, network in contract_interface['networks'].items():

        address=web3.Web3.toChecksumAddress(network['address']),
        abi=contract_interface['abi'])

```

        address=network['address'],  # Address all lowercased

```

The monkey patch is to simply ignore the EIP checksum validation.

Not sure what is the best way to fix it. Simply ignoring checksum validation seems not right. I'm glad to discuss.

issue_title: ResourceWarnings from the web clients session
issue_body:
* Version: 4.2.1
* Python: 3.6
* OS: osx


### What was wrong?

We converted the raiden RPCClient to use the web3 client. Now we get a lot of `ResourceWarning:`s  about unclosed sockets in the web client.

Example:
```
raiden/tests/integration/test_blockchainservice.py::test_channel_with_self[0-1]
  /home/travis/virtualenv/python3.6.3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/web3/utils/request.py:14: ResourceWarning: unclosed <socket object, fd=19, family=2, type=2049, proto=6>
    _session_cache[cache_key] = requests.Session()
  /home/travis/virtualenv/python3.6.3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/web3/utils/request.py:14: ResourceWarning: unclosed <socket object, fd=17, family=2, type=2049, proto=6>
    _session_cache[cache_key] = requests.Session()
  /home/travis/virtualenv/python3.6.3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/web3/utils/request.py:14: ResourceWarning: unclosed <socket object, fd=10, family=2, type=2049, proto=6>
    _session_cache[cache_key] = requests.Session()
  /home/travis/virtualenv/python3.6.3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/web3/utils/request.py:14: ResourceWarning: unclosed <socket object, fd=20, family=2, type=2049, proto=6>
    _session_cache[cache_key] = requests.Session()
```

For details see here:
https://travis-ci.org/raiden-network/raiden/jobs/386089172#L895


### How can it be fixed?

The sessions should be properly closed.

issue_title: Can we have an option to specify ACE's path for compile as well?
Improve handling of rentrancies in feature structures
issue_body:
Currently coreferences in TDL are just catalogued for later analysis, and only for TdlType classes. Non-TDL TFSs (like those in UDF tokens) may have similar reentrancies, so they should be handled all the way down in tfs.py.

Furthermore, beyond just tracking where coreferences have been defined, it would be nice to actually unify the feature structures (although subsumptive unification would be tricky).

issue_title: Error with ACE and --tsdb-stdout with long unparsable sentences
issue_body:
When ACE v0.9.24 is installed, pyDelphin defaults to using the --tsdb-stdout option in order to get more information out of ACE. NOTEs and other messages to stderr are printed as normal. When a long unparsable sentence causes a NOTE to be generated, this message may be flushed to stderr before the stdout line has finished being written. Because pyDelphin interleaves stdout and stderr (in order to properly read the output; especially for generation), the NOTE on stderr makes the s-expression on stdout unreadable by pyDelphin.

Some solutions include:
- de-interleave stdout and stderr when `--tsdb-stdout` is used
- change ACE so it only flushes stderr after writing a newline to stdout
- load ACE for every input sentence so output can be read until EOF
- ignore stderr entirely when `--tsdb-stdout` is used

The example sentence is:

```
这个 学期 我 有 四 门 课 ， 不  太 忙 ， 我 每天 十点一 刻 去 上课 ， 十一点二十分 下课 ， 然后 ， 我 喜欢 去 图书馆 看书 ， 下午 下课 以后 ， 我 常 和 朋友 一起 去 打球 。
```

It was parsed using a recent version of [Zhong](https://github.com/delph-in/zhong) (the grammar at zhong/cmn/zhs/)

This seems to be a regression introduced in 4.3.0 since I could not reproduce this for 4.2.16.

issue_title: multiuser install - "text file is busy" when activating environment
issue_body:
Today, from a user account, I did

```
$ source activate anarel-1.0.0
```
and got
```
-bash: /central-install/miniconda2/bin/conda: /central-install/miniconda2/bin/python: bad interpreter: No such file or directory
```
I did it a second time and it worked.

we see this kind of thing on our filesystem when we do thinks like modify then install a file and immediately run the file from the new location, sleeping a second in between those steps fixes it. I think it is a problem with our nfs filesystem, but I was surprised to see it when I activate an environment. I know you guys are looking at multi-user conda, so wanted to raise the issue - to make conda robust on different filesystems, keep things like source activate as lightweight as possible? (Or tell us to fix our filesystem :)

issue_title: Incorrect result for np.rint on Win32, numpy 1.10.1, MKL
issue_body:
Sorry for the repetition - I just reported this to numpy : https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/6685

However, it seems to be an issue specific to MKL, and therefore to the (only?) conda numpy packages for Windows 32-bit.

With 32-bit numpy + MKL on Windows:

```
>>> import numpy as np
>>> ds = 4607998452777363968
>>> int(float(ds))
4607998452777363968L
>>> int(np.rint(ds))
4607998452777363456L
```

I do not get this error from:

* conda 32-bit + numpy 1.9.3;
* conda 64-bit + numpy 1.10.1;
* Python.org Python + the sourceforge numpy 1.10.1 superpack installers;

I do get the same error from the (now removed?) Christoph Gohlke numpy 1.10.1 32-bit MKL wheels.

issue_title: Redefinition of SHELL variable causing issues
issue_body:
https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/bin/activate#L4-L13

Since we're all sourcing this file, perhaps you should consider using a throw-away variable here instead of overwriting an important environment variable in a user's shell. A variable like `_SHELL="bash"` or `shell="bash"` would be fine, but not definitely not `SHELL="bash"`. 

I've run into a situation with some C code failing to execute `system` calls because `SHELL` is no longer an absolute path after running `source activate [env_name]`. The redefinition from `/bin/bash` to `bash` sticks until you either (i) open a new terminal or (ii) manually reset the value.

issue_title: WebSocket close by remote server results in non-graceful disconnection
issue_body:
In 0.2.2 I'm observing that a WebSocket close initiated by the remote server results in a non-graceful `Disconnected` event.  As far as I can tell this is a regression relative to 0.2.1.

This appears to be due to a small change in the main session `run()` loop, where an `is_active` check was removed in the no data case.  I believe this case is triggered when the socket is closed because the selector triggers as readable, but there is no data.  See the change [here](https://github.com/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/commit/fe33e3f032e206cc5386fb839f1cefdbd3c63b37#diff-47e18c92a2e61d31deb894529bda889dL398) on lines 398-400 on the left-hand side.  Restoring these lines appears to fix the issue.

I'm attaching a test case that should produce non-graceful behavior.  This is based on `test_simple_run()` from test_session.py, but adds the necessary bytes for a close to the payload.  Also, note the `session.websocket.state.session = session` code on line 45 of the diff file.  This feels pretty hackish but without it I ran into a different issue about not being able to set the `sent_close_time` in `WebSocket.close()` because the session was not defined in the WebSocket state.


[repro_test.diff](https://github.com/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/files/2000852/repro_test.diff.txt)

issue_title: Elasticsearch 5.0.0 does not work with sniffing
issue_body:
See https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/21292.

Elasticsearch 5.0.0 does not return the http_address field on _nodes/_all/clear. Therefore, https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/5.0.1/elasticsearch/transport.py#L206 needs to use `/_nodes/_all/http` or some other mechanism to determine which nodes have http interfaces.

This bug causes causes all sniffing to fail against a 5.0.0 server:
```python
>>> import elasticsearch
>>> from pprint import pprint
>>> elasticsearch.__version__
(5, 0, 1)

issue_title: Inconsistent error thrown when naming a missing blob using a pattern
issue_body:
If I try to delete a blob with a pattern that doesn't match anything:

    az storage blob copy start-batch --destination-container="foo" --source-container "bar" \
                                     --pattern "foo/*"

or even

    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo*"

I get an expected empty list of copied paths.  However, if there is no
`*` in the pattern I get an error:

    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo"
    → Failed to copy blob foo to container deleted.

This is inconsistent: I expected this case to also produce an empty list.

It is particularly bad when viewing storage as a hierarchy of
directories and files: in such a case, to copy `foo`, I need to do two
copies:

    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo"
    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo/*"

because the union of both cannot be done in a single glob (and using
`foo*` will also remove, for example `foo1`, which is wrong).

issue_title: Existing update commands should expose generic update fields
issue_body:
For consistency across commands, and to fill any gaps that may occur when a service exposes new functionality before a CLI rev, we should expose the generic update parameters (`--add --set --remove`) on each of our existing update commands.

The generic update is currently assembled from a setter and a getter, with common code to handle the generic parameters. We should be able to relatively easily insert our existing custom commands into that flow.

The recommended execution order would be:

1. Call the getter to retrieve the object.
2. Pass the object into the custom command (this will require removing the getter and setter statements from the existing custom command and adding a parameter for the object)
3. Pass the object on to the standard generic update code.
4. Pass the object to the setter.

issue_title: [Create Commands] Flags will be confusing in SDK
issue_body:
Example:
Create a vm from SDK (not CLI) using the Smart Create command for vm.
There is a availability_set parameter and a availability_set_type parameter.  
Set the availability_set parameter to an availability set ID.
Run the command.

Expected: vm is added to the availability set
Actual: the vm is not added to the availability set because the availability_set_type parameter was set to null (which defaults to 'none').

Possible solution: we make the flags (which are a limitation of ARM templates) required so users must fill them out and then look for the related field to set a value to.  We would need to hide that required-ness on the CLI side.

Command

issue_title: Compound physics Armature Actors
issue_body:
So I have found an easy way to get an accurate physics shape for actors in blender

I have a compound root object - the armature to be animated parents to this,
each bone that is to have physics has a 'bone tag' parented to it, and a shild shape
that approximates the shape of the bone

every frame I remove parent on all the child shapes, move them to where the bone tag is, and match it's rotation, then compound parent the child shape to the root,

this allows my actors to go up and down stairs by applying a forward servo motion while playing a stair walk animation.

this system works great, but when a child shape is contacting, and is moved, produces no friction,

I purpose a build in system that does all this under the hood but also computes the friction during the move maybe using substeps?

here is the system in action - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cx_9F0RNtzc
however she will not walk forward only by playing an animation - I have to combine the animation with a forward sero motion.

issue_title: DeprecationWarning: invalid escape sequence \w (aiohttp/client_reqrep.py:41)
issue_body:
## Long story short

Running python3.6 with all warnings enabled, and before aiohttp 3.3.2 has been compiled to .pyc files:

```
py.warnings WARNING  /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/client_reqrep.py:41: DeprecationWarning: invalid escape sequence \w
  json_re = re.compile('^application/(?:[\w.+-]+?\+)?json')
```

The fix is to make the regex a raw string, i.e. 

```
json_re = re.compile(r'^application/(?:[\w.+-]+?\+)?json')
```

issue_title: Distinguish Between Administrative Powers (instructor/graders)
issue_body: At the moment, both an instructor and their graders have the same administrative powers when grading. As far as the average assignment goes, this is probably fine, however it would might be preferable if an instructor could block their graders from altering certain assignment (e.g. tests). As far as I'm aware, if a grader were inclined to artificially boost the test grades of a particular student, there wouldn't be anything to stop them.

issue_title: Dry run upload
issue_body:
A user requested to see what would be uploaded before running the upload process.
We could add a --dryrun flag:
```
ddsclient upload -p MouseProject results/ --dryrun
```
This would print out what files will be uploaded.
We have a summary that already prints for regular upload.

issue_title: Data Delivery wiki page review comments
issue_body:
1. In [_Delivering a project_](https://github.com/Duke-GCB/DukeDSClient/wiki/Data-Delivery#delivering-a-project), it says _This step is customizable, but ..._. It's not clear what the _but_ means - are the parts following the _but_ those that aren't customizable, or are those the default steps, or something else?
2. In the same section, does the same disclaimer apply to specifying recipient by email than above for sharing? Either way, since the limitation with using email is specifically mentioned earlier on, it should be clarified here whether the limitation does or does not apply here as well.
3. In [_Notes_](https://github.com/Duke-GCB/DukeDSClient/wiki/Data-Delivery#notes-1) under _Delivering a project_, rather than "Notes" this seems to be how to customize delivery. So change heading to reflect that?
4. I'm wondering whether it's worth to replace `NetID` with something more generic (`userID`?), and then say somewhere that at Duke this is the NetID.

I've also [made a couple of smaller changes](https://github.com/Duke-GCB/DukeDSClient/wiki/Data-Delivery/_compare/32cd9d2ba1f421a108963f059366801dd1192099...99bd0a9223eeaec02aa4cba9ab85ea26a4e2c1b1) directly.

issue_title: Code Review notes from February 24, 2016
issue_body:
Documentation / initial impressions:
- Clarify hack and apiexplorer, e.g: To use DukeDSClient, you must supply an authentication token. This token identifies you with the service, and can be generated by visiting LINK and logging in with ACCOUNT. Note that tokens obtained this way are only valid for 2 hours. Work is underway to improve this process and obtain longer-lived tokens.
- `ddsclient -h` fails with an error if token is not set. It doesn't seem to care WHAT the token is set to, but fails if it is not set. Help should still be accessible even if the env var is set.
- Unclear from help how adduser argument works. ADD_USERNAME suggests a username but help doc says its "their full name". Also, it's not clear if this can be used without uploading data or what..
- "Where projectname is the name of the project to add folder too." - text is unclear
- Why not change `--allowsymlink` to `--followsymlinks`
- What's the recommended way to run the tests from a command-line?

General usability:
- The client is functional and allowed me to upload a directory. :100: 
- upload is the implicit action, but there seem to be other actions too. I'd suggest the first argument be the verb, e.g.
`ddsclient upload -p "project" dir1 dir2`
- I think that adding an explicit action as well as flags for projects (`-p`) or users (`-u`) will make the tool more understandable as additional functions are added (download, share, add_user). 
- Report formatting is a little tight and could use some whitespace in between the lines. As a next step,
I think a structured report (e.g. JSON, XML, or YAML including the file checksums) would be great for archival or transformation into other formats.

Code-level
- Do you have any thoughts on extracting constants out of classes (e.g. the default URL or bytes_per_chunk)? I tend to prefer that constants be pulled out, so they're not hiding deep in code.
- Nice use of visitor pattern, but it could use some high-level explanation on how it gets going.
- some minor spelling issues: e.g. tuple not tupil
- If visit_project's parent is always None, can this parameter be removed?
- Standardize variable names - In some cases, a DDSApi is `dsa` and in some cases it's `data_service`. `data_service` is more descriptive.
- A `LocalContent` object is initialized with kind set to project. This seems important for accept to get started, please document this behavior in the initializer
- `RemoteContentSender` and `_send_different_local_content()` seem pretty important, and could use  more explanation (e.g. first we traverse the local content tree to get total file and folder counts/sizes. Second, ...)
- At what point in the `DDSClient.run()` will a failed upload be caught? If a chunk fails, can it be retried automatically?

Unusual behavior:
- `DDSClient` has a single `run()` function that performs a specific series of tasks (primarily uploading). The naming is confusing - and if the client will have other entrypoints, it's probably best to rename run() to indicate what it will do (e.g. `upload()`)
- `_read_file()` in `remotestore.py` is misleading, since it's not reading a file, it's more populating metadata

issue_title: Containers and higher-order grads.
Gradient of numpy.maximum
issue_body:
The gradient of numpy.maximum is inconsistent:

`autograd.grad(numpy.maximum, 0)(1., 1.)` returns `1.0`

`autograd.grad(numpy.maximum, 1)(1., 1.)` returns `0.0`

I do realise that the gradient of the maximum function is discontinuous at this point (when the two arguments are equal), but since the function is symmetric, it would make sense for the gradients to be symmetric as well.  The former behaviour (returning `1.0`) is probably more helpful, e.g. in the case of gradient descent.

issue_title: can't send message (test account) to my number
Test and document usage of pip to install and generate wheels
STACKS: What happens if we're missing an address in the genesis block?
issue_body:
@kantai, @jeffdomke, and @jessesoslow raised a critical concern for deploying the Stacks token:  what happens if, somehow, we don't get somebody's account address by the time the genesis block goes live?  This could happen if the token buyer loses their email address, or the token buyer dies before the genesis block launches and their estate executor doesn't know to send us an address.

The solution we arrived at was to create an `ACCOUNT_TRANSFER` opcode.  The `ACCOUNT_TRANSFER` opcode would do the following:
* Transfer all tokens from the sender address to the receiver address
* Transfer all token vesting schedules from the sender address to the receiver address (so the recipient would vest the tokens instead)
* Transfer the transfer lock-up period from the sender address to the receiver address (so the recipient cannot spend tokens until the lock-up period passes).
* Remove the sender address from the accounts table.

The `ACCOUNT_TRANSFER` operation could only be used **once** over the lifetime of an account.  Once an account is transferred, it cannot be transferred again.

When the genesis block launches, all accounts that have addresses would be non-transferrable by default.  Only accounts for which we **do not yet have an address for** would be transferrable.  We would hold onto a private key for each such account, and issue a `ACCOUNT_TRANSFER` transaction to send the account to the rightful owner once they reach out to us and give us an address.

@shea256 @muneeb-ali Thoughts or concerns?

issue_title: Add test case UUIDs to generated xunit files
issue_body:
Given a `xunit` file, create a new method to parse through the source code for automated tests, and update the XML file to include the test case UUID for each test found. For example, given the following (snippet) xunit file:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?><testsuite errors="3" failures="2" name="pytest" skips="0" tests="3" time="4157.148">
<testcase classname="tests.foreman.api.test_syncplan.SyncPlanSynchronizeTestCase" file="tests/foreman/api/test_syncplan.py" line="609" name="test_positive_synchronize_custom_product_future_sync_date" time="981.63787818">
```

The new method should look at the source code for the test named `test_positive_synchronize_custom_product_future_sync_date` in the file `tests/foreman/api/test_syncplan.py` and extract the `uuid` from it.

From this `uuid` we would have to query `Polarion` to fetch the test case so that we can extract the test case `polarion-testcase-id`.

 Once this information is made available, we would then generate a brand new xml file containing the `polarion-testcase-id` as part of a new `<properties><property>` block:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?><testsuite errors="3" failures="2" name="pytest" skips="0" tests="3" time="4157.148">
<testcase classname="tests.foreman.api.test_syncplan.SyncPlanSynchronizeTestCase" file="tests/foreman/api/test_syncplan.py" line="609" name="test_positive_synchronize_custom_product_future_sync_date" time="981.63787818">
    <properties> 
        <property name="polarion-testcase-id" value="RHSAT6-4987"/> 
    </properties> 
</testcase>
```

issue_title: How to create individual log files for each worker
issue_body:
I'm trying to figure out how individual log files can be created for each worker, in the same manor that pytest --log-file does when not using xdist.

Since the basic pytest --log-file opens with write, not append (see https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3813), there does not seem to be a way of getting a complete log file for a run using xdist with multiple workers.  However, even if it could be opened in append mode, the individual worker IDs would not be present, and having everything mixed together can be more difficult for troubleshooting.

I'm hoping that there is some way of creating a log file for each worker, but I'm not really sure how to do that, if it's even possible.  I tried setting up a logger in pytest_configure, but worker_id does not seem to be available at that time.

issue_title: tos as url or true in renewal/:hostname.conf
issue_body:
I see that `:conf/renewal/:hostname.conf`has a field `tos = True` and I couldn't find anything out about it in the docs, but I'd like to suggest that it allow a string url or True.

The purpose of the string url is that if the tos changes and the user has decided (in some user interface) that the would not like certificates to renew until they have had the opportunity to view the revised terms to which they will legally be bound that the renewal should fail.

I know this seems unlikely, but there are some people who take this stuff very seriously and others [who don't](http://www.foxnews.com/tech/2010/04/15/online-shoppers-unknowingly-sold-souls.html).

As of the current date I think it's generally agreed upon that Mozilla and the EFF have goals and values that align with their customers. And of course they always will be, forever (maybe).

But other companies may step up to the plate that do not have their customers' best interests in mind - those who use free ssl as a marketing tactic and later abuse their customers privacy or other rights. It's uncomfortable to unwittingly sell your immortal soul, especially automatically.

* http://www.foxnews.com/tech/2010/04/15/online-shoppers-unknowingly-sold-souls.html

issue_title: [Ask Purpose of Access Control on P2P Topic]
issue_body:
Hello, Gene

I have question regarding purpose of access control on `P2P` topic. So what is the purpose of it anyway? 

The reason why I ask this question is because I found that the purpose stated in documentation is somewhat _contradict_ with the implementation written on code. So with this question I would like to validate what I found because I think it is very fundamental.

In documentation [(see here)](https://github.com/tinode/chat/blob/master/API.md#access-control) I found that the purpose of it is for managing presence + banning user from initiating or continuing `P2P` conversation. So if there are `user1` & `user2` which have `P2P` connection, then after sometime `user2` would like to block message from `user1`, then `user2` would be just only need to remove `“W”` permission from `user1`.

But based on current implementation it is not possible to achieve that purpose. The reason is in current implementation it is not possible to change others permission on `P2P` topic, it is just for setting user own permission for filtering the data retrieved by user itself _(which I personally think is somewhat useless on this context)_. 

So in above case, `user2` would only able to manage its own permission, so does with `user1`, so they cannot block each other in case they want to do it.

So which purpose is true? Managing the `P2P` connection or just for filtering data retrieved from the context? Or maybe you have another design decision?

Thanks a lot, Gene.

issue_title: Fastavro fallback doesn't prevent errors
issue_body:
It looks like the intent in `message_serializer.py` is to use `fastavro` and then to fallback to `avro.io` if it is not available.

Because `fastavro` doesn't seem to be able to fully replace `avro.io` and can fail deserialising messaged that are otherwise deserialised fine with `avro.io` the fallback should be more reliable.

**Currently**:
if `fastavro` is present, create a function using it and register as a deserialiser, otherwise register a function that uses `avro.io`

**Ideally**:
Give users an option on whether to use `fastavro` or not by not including it in dependencies in `setup.py` so `pip` does not automatically install one

**Acceptable**:
Register a decode function that tries `fastavro` first, and if it fails fallbacks to `avro.io` for every message.

issue_title: Provide an easy way to preload some packages that exist on pypi.python.org
issue_body:
One of the most important advantages of having a private pypiserver is that you decouple yourself from the dependency of pypi.python.org which can go down or receive changes that may impact you.

We need a documented way to do something like:

* upload latest version of package `foobar` from pypi.python.org to our internal server
* upload foobar==1.2.3 from pypi to our internal server
* remove package version from internal server

Please note that fallback feature cannot be used because it does affect all packages. We need to be able to control what and when gets updated.

The workaround at this moment is to manually download these files from pypi and to put them on disk, which is an ugly manual process.

issue_title: Setup builds so xfail doesn't count towards code coverage
issue_body:
We've lately added quite a few `xfail` tests as a sort of "to do", but I realized that this may cause the `codecov` metrics to be less meaningful than one might hope. The reason being that `xfail` tests can fail for *any* reason, including that they went down some random buggy code path whose *proper* behavior is not actually covered, and that buggy code path threw an actual bug we should be aware of. As such, I'd rather code executed as the result of `xfail` tests *not* contribute to the code coverage. (They should still be *run* because I'd like to know if they turn from xfail to xpass).

If there's not just some standard `pytest-coverage` or `coverage` option that does this, I think it's simple enough to set up by just running `pytest` with all `xfail` tests skipped with `coverage` on, then running it a second time with coverage off and *only* `xfail` tests included.

issue_title: Inconsistent behaviour when dealing with dates close to year 0
issue_body:
I wrote the following simple test using the python-hypothesis library. It generates dates, renders them as strings, then unpacks them using `parser.parse()` and checks the date survived the trip:

```python
from dateutil.parser import parse

@given(datetimes())
def test_round_trip(d):
    assert parse(str(d)) == d
```

If you try enough examples: this test fails:

* Pass in `d=datetime.datetime(4, 4, 1, 0, 0)` – i.e., April 1st in the year 4
* Casting to `str()`, this becomes `'0004-04-01 00:00:00'`
* When unpacked with `parse()`, it becomes `datetime.datetime(1, 4, 4, 0, 0)` – i.e., April 4th in the year 1. Mismatch!

The same problem occurs for all `datetime.datetime(M, M, 1, 0, 0)` for `1 < M <= 12`.

Given the four-digit year, I think this string is fairly unambiguous, but it seems to be getting confused somewhere, and treating the `-01` as the year. But [two-digit years are supposed](https://labix.org/python-dateutil#head-19e5d3331806e55ec2ba1b9e39458784cc0a0004) to be within 50 years of the current year:

> **Converting two digit years**

> When a two digit year is found, it is processed considering the current year, so that the computed year is never more than 49 years after then current year, nor 50 years before the current year. In other words, if we are in year 2003, and the year 30 is found, it will be considered as 2030, but if the year 60 is found, it will be considered 1960.

So if it really is treating `-01` as the year, then based on this rule it should be returning `datetime.datetime(2001, 4, 4, 0, 0)`.

issue_title: Dockerized tools fail if they don't respect TMPDIR
issue_body:
As of e645adecab98417f96e7676ab54e0b60b042da61, cwltool sets `--read-only=true` when running docker. This makes the container's file system read-only, excepting only volumes attached as **rw** at runtime. I think this is a great idea, but have run into issues with `/tmp`.

Since `/tmp` is not a volume, it is read-only inside the container. I do see that the `TMPDIR` variable is set to a **rw** volume, but many tools don't respect `TMPDIR`, still try to write to `/tmp`, and now fail.

I can probably work around this in a few cases, but I figured it was worth discussing. Is there a reason the job's temp directory is mounted at `/tmp/job_tmp` instead of just `/tmp`?

Scenario: Bonsai.io is a hosted ES provider whose SSL certs support TLS only (woo!).

Sadly, elasticsearch defaults to SSLv23 (actually not sure if that's a Python 2.7 default or ES), leading to an SSL protocol error when using pyelasticsearch. However, the SSL version can be chosen ([docs](http://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.org/en/master/transports.html#urllib3httpconnection)).

issue_title: termios.error on connection lost
issue_body:
Hi,

I am not sure where to report this, if here or to pyserial, but i am sure, that you will know what to do with it...

While playing with `pyserial.aio` and try to build serial server, which waits for USB/UART adapter to be connected, then process incoming data and if the connection will lost, then again wait for adapter. I start with base example there with virtual serial port (created via `socat`) and all seems to work, except situation when i simulate connection lost (exit the `socat`), where i got exception, which i was not able to catch:

```python
Exception in callback SerialTransport._call_connection_lost(SerialExcepti...s on port?)',))
handle: <Handle SerialTransport._call_connection_lost(SerialExcepti...s on port?)',))>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.4/site-packages/serial/aio.py", line 346, in _call_connection_lost
    self._serial.flush()
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.4/site-packages/serial/serialposix.py", line 550, in flush
    termios.tcdrain(self.fd)
termios.error: (5, 'Input/output error')
```
This exception causes, that the `loop` is not closed inside the `connection_lost` method in `Output` class (because it is not called at all), the connection stays open and when the port is created again, it is not more connected to program.

After small investigation in the `/usr/lib64/python3.4/site-packages/serial/aio.py` file (from `pyserial` 3.1.1 on my Funtoo Linux) i found that in the `_call_connection_lost` (the `SerialTransport` class) is called `self._serial.flush()`, where exception occurs (because there is no more this port in system). After i changed the line 345:
```python
self._serial.flush()
```
to ignore this exception (because it is expected):
```python
try:
    self._serial.flush()
except termios.error:
    pass
```
(and appropriate `import termios` of course) all seems to work as expected, i.e. the `connection_lost` method of the `Output` class is called and loop is closed.

I am not Python nor pyserialúasyncio expert, then i am not sure if this is solution or workaround, but i see that similar solution is
